novel_features.name: Local training database
novel_features.description: Sync your full activity, wellness, event, workout and gear history into a local SQLite database for offline search and SQL.
novel_features.rationale: Every existing intervals.icu tool proxies live API calls; none persists a local store, so none supports offline query or cross-season analysis.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first so search/sql/form work offline without hammering the API on every query.

novel_features.name: Fitness & form trend
novel_features.description: Compute CTL (fitness), ATL (fatigue) and TSB/form from your synced activity load and show the trend.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local time series of training load that only exists once activities are persisted locally; no other CLI computes it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to answer 'am I fresh enough to race / overreaching?' without opening the web UI.

novel_features.name: Season curve comparison
novel_features.description: Compare best power/pace/HR curves between two date ranges from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining best-effort curves across arbitrary historical ranges held locally; the API returns one range at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to quantify season-over-season fitness change for a given duration.

novel_features.name: Wellness vs load correlation
novel_features.description: Correlate HRV / resting HR / sleep against training load over a window from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining the wellness and activity time series locally; no single API call returns the correlation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to spot whether HRV/resting-HR are tracking accumulated fatigue.

novel_features.name: Calendar catch-up window
novel_features.description: Show planned workouts, completed activities, and what was missed within a recent time window.
novel_features.rationale: Requires time-windowed aggregation across events and activities that no single endpoint provides.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for a quick 'what happened / what's coming' digest after time away.

novel_features.name: Gear mileage rollup
novel_features.description: Roll up distance/time per gear component against reminders to flag what needs service or replacement.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregating gear usage and reminders locally into one view; the API exposes them piecemeal.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to catch chain/tyre/shoe replacement thresholds before they are overdue.

narrative.headline: The first intervals.icu CLI with a local training database — offline search, SQL, and fitness/form analytics no other tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Sync your activities, wellness, calendar, workouts and gear into local SQLite, then search, run SQL, and compute fitness/form trends offline. Wraps the full intervals.icu REST API for live reads and writes, and adds analytics every existing MCP server and wrapper leaves on the table.

quickstart.comment: Check auth and API reachability first.
Populate the local store so offline commands work.

troubleshoots.fix: Set INTERVALS_ICU_API_KEY to your key from intervals.icu Settings - Developer; username is the literal API_KEY.
Run sync first; those commands read the local store, not the live API.

narrative.when_to_use: Use when an agent or user needs to query intervals.icu training data programmatically: pulling activities, wellness, calendar and workouts, computing fitness/form, or comparing performance curves across seasons. Best when repeated queries make a local synced store worthwhile.
anti_triggers: Do not use for real-time device streaming during a ride
Do not use to edit another athlete's data without coach permissions
Do not use as a replacement for the web UI's interactive charts

recipes.title: Season-over-season power
recipes.explanation: Compare recent best power against a year ago, selecting just the peak arrays.

recipes.title: Fresh enough to race?
recipes.explanation: Show six weeks of fitness/fatigue/form to judge taper readiness.

recipes.title: Catch up after a break
recipes.explanation: Digest of planned, completed and missed sessions over two weeks.
